evidence: TASK-053 verified; behavior-preserving refinement complete. buildFreshRegistryWithChain(substrateDir, chain) lives in migration-registry-loader.ts beside buildRegistryFromSubstrateForDir — substrate on-disk decls first (the substrate decl wins a (schemaName, fromVersion) collision), the chain's absent edges merged, migrationFnFor per decl, cache-untouched, no disk writes — and the three former in-memory seeding blocks (simulateResyncOutcome, validateBlockItemsAgainstCatalog, resolveBlocked) call it; createRegistry no longer appears in index.ts. The marker sentinel contract has one source: MARKER_OPEN/MARKER_CLOSE token constants with MARKER_LINE_RE (+ the /m twin) derived from them; composeMarkerText builds its lines from the tokens and every detection/strip site uses the derived regex — the seven-character literals remain only in the constants and user-facing display text. The on-disk appendMigrationDeclForDir loops and the live cache-warming getProjectMigrationRegistryForDir call are byte-unchanged. Behavior preservation: the full test suite passed with ZERO test-file edits (the diff is exactly migration-registry-loader.ts + index.ts + one CHANGELOG line); build + check + parity-check green; the runtime blocked -> markers -> byte-exact restore -> fix -> resolve-blocked -> in-sync loop converges on the refactored dist. Re-audit greps confirm no residual seeding block, the literal placement, and the untouched out-of-scope paths.
